key_insight: A clinically guided multimodal model that combines ultrasound images, text descriptions, and structured clinical data improves fine-grained differentiation between breast fibroadenoma and phyllodes tumors.
clinical_relevance: Better preoperative classification may help reduce misclassification of borderline or benign phyllodes tumors as fibroadenoma and support more informed surgical planning.
method: Built the FAPT-M Dataset with 910 pathology-confirmed patients and multimodal inputs.
Used DenseNet for image encoding, CLIP-inspired text encoding, and lightweight clinical feature encoding.
Added clinical-conditioned modulation, cross-modal Transformer fusion, and dual-path representation learning.
evidence_signal: On patient-level five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 77.64% accuracy, 73.38% F1-score, and 89.74% AUC, outperforming representative CNN, Transformer, and vision-language baselines.
watch_item: The dataset comes from a specific cohort and the approach will need external validation and real-world workflow testing before broader deployment.
paper_title: Multimodal Fusion for Fine-Grained Classification of Breast Fibroadenoma and Phyllodes Tumors
